author: @rebelforgod
text:
I'm hoping to get performance numbers on your stacked graphene-paper aluminum-cells, hoping it's at least 20% as efficient as a car battery, and lasts 50 charge cycles or more.
If so, then I'd like to make 8 3in x 1ft cylindrical cells from whole rolls each of aluminum foil, then stack them into an 8ft PVC pipe, making 12v Power Tubes, for my RV's solar system. Another way would be to bind them into a block of parallel cylinders,,, like some performance car batteries are.
On your Moped, why don't you find the power rating of the headlamp, then time how long that takes to drain your battery, compared to the factory battery?
It seems this would give you some quick hard numbers to use for comparing your garage-made battery with a commercial grade one.

replies.text: ok

author: @veronicathecow
text: Robert, I can't remember what they are called but there was a small device like a reed switch (also a similar size) that only cost a few pounds. Basically it had a small blob of mercury in it and some electrolyte and markings on the side. It is a very simple way of getting a rough idea of how long a device has been in operation. The electrolysis formed gasses and moved the blob of mercury along the graduated glass case. Just set the current via a resister put across your motor and you can easily keep a track of how long it has been running. Best wishes.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: oh - nice idea - i will try and hunt one out

author: @richardsandwell2285
text: Once all Cars and Motorbikes have clean and durable battery storage, we can finally start to say goodbye to dependence on fossil fuels, we can generate electricity by clean renewable methods and store it in batteries at peak production times. It will not be popular with the oil companies, but it is time the world moved forward. Its no good for any ones health be they rich or poor, they both get cancer and die from polluted air and water supplies. The world must develop a different economy that works for all not just an elite minority.

author: @BenCollinsDigital
text: I think the method of real-use experimentation is just perfect. I've been meaning to ask: With all the news about exploding Lithium batteries causing burns, do you think there is an equivalent risk to graphene batteries or less of a risk? On your logo (I design logos as part of my job sometimes): The intro is fun. I think the symbol is appropriately "vehicular" and I am fond of the idea of a brand that can be 3 Dimensional.  As logos go, it's very tall which would create issues on things like a mobile website if not reconfigured (like... reduce the symbol and put it off to the left for example).  I think this is Futura, which has a very hot "W" but I wish the two bars of the "F" were extended slightly to match the width of the "G".

replies.author: @BenCollinsDigital
replies.text: Just to be extra clear: the "mobile website" logo I was referring to would be a variation of what I see here; I was not suggesting it replace the "default" brand.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: the risk is equivalent if the electrolyte is organic - we use a water based electrolyte especially to eliminate that risk - so in this battery that risk doesn't exist - nice comments on the logo - cheers mate

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: i get it mate - cheers

author: @VICTORIA-SAYERS
text:
Hi Mate, glad to see things are going well with the Battery and the Bike and thanks for the update. P. S. Mate, need your advice, with all the talk now about the Nuclear Waste Battery in Diamonds that can last 5,000 Years, do you think you, me and all us Battery/Generator makers should just throw in the Towel? I mean with a Battery that can be small enough to fit in Pace Makers with no Moving Parts and capable of lasting thousands of Years that doesn't leave much Room for the rest of the Industry to stay afloat, so was just wondering what your thoughts on this might be. 

https://youtu.be/0R7bL2Q7BK8

author: @FredGandt
text:
Logo feedback (as requested): It _feels_ to me, akin to those production studio splashes that follow the credits of early evening American TV serials. Remember the guy at the typewriter who throws the finished page over his shoulder, and it swishes to the floor where it effects a pile of other pages such that they form a _"C"_ - at the end of The A Team?
Kinda like that.

\o/ my memory served me well: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=2F-KGNxvL4g <-- A Team end credits.

A bit _"glam"_ and _"ITV Telethon Special"_ backdrop. All that's missing are the words _"Phone lines now open"_ flashing at the bottom of the screen.
;-)

It looks (and sounds) like an outsourced, professional, swishy, animated, corporate logo, which may or may not be what you're going for.
I think it would be pretty much ideal for the start of one of those gordy tech presentations Microsoft and Nvidia are keen on, where people dressed as casually as a plain clothes cop from an 80's TV show, pace back and forth on a stage telling the audience how the wall of stock photos of happy attractive people are because of _"Teh New Tech Revolution Thing [we made and will sell you]"_...

In this context (a quick _"'ow do"_), it sticks out like a sore thumb.
_ _ _ _

You wanted an honest opinion right?
No?!
Ah, well, erm; *it's awesome! :-D*

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: i understand mate - but to be honest i quite like it - but i have questionable taste sometimes lol

replies.author: @FredGandt
replies.text:
As long as you're happy Rob, that's what ultimately matters. I'm just responding to your request.

It's very swish.
To put my opinion into some perspective: I like Cody's (Lab) intro(s. There have been a few). I like that he made it out of the things he had to hand.
I like that it's rough, ready and utterly unique to him and what he does.

As a reluctant (more so each day) web dev the phenomenon of practically generic beautification really bugs me. Templated websites with the same f*cking stock photos and super stylish GUI wizardry that leads to finding no useful information...
It's really *easy* these days to dress up in pretty clothes and flounce around (figuratively), and a lot of people fall for it, but I'll always prefer home spun individuality.
_ _ _ _

The bike's wicked BTW :-)
